issues: The headline 'Sockeyes Win Overtime Thriller Over Broncos' does not appear in the OCR — there is no identifiable headline in the extracted text; the AI appears to have fabricated it.

The draft describes this as a 'Centennial Cup western final' but the OCR says it is a 'Tier 2 western final series' (the Abbott Cup/western final stage), NOT the Centennial Cup final. The Centennial Cup is the national championship; the western final is the Abbott Cup round — and notably the opponent is Humboldt Broncos, who are the Saskatchewan (not national) champion at this stage.
The date April 23 1987 is inconsistent with the Centennial Cup final (which was May 9 1987); this game is from the Abbott Cup (western final) series, not the Centennial Cup.
Opponent's scorer surname spelled 'Chamberlin' in draft but OCR reads 'Chamberlin' — acceptable; however McDougall's first name 'Bill' is confirmed by OCR as 'Bill McDougall', so that is correct.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Tony Bobbitt scored at 1:04 of overtime as the Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Humboldt Broncos 4-3 to take a 2-1 lead in the Tier 2 western final (Abbott Cup) series.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Tony Bobbitt's overtime goal at 1:04 gave the Richmond Sockeyes a 4-3 win over the Humboldt Broncos in Game 3 of the best-of-seven Tier 2 western final series, played Wednesday night in Humboldt. The victory put Richmond ahead 2-1 in the series, with Game 4 scheduled for Saturday in Richmond. Dave Tomlinson, Mike Claringbull, and Rob Clarke also scored for the Sockeyes, who were outshot 39-23; Bill McDougall (2) and Curtis Chamberlin replied for the Saskatchewan champion Broncos. A brief note in the same article reports that the Pembroke Lumber Kings led their eastern final series 3-1 after an 8-2 win over Nickel Centre.
review.notes: The AI draft incorrectly labels this a "Centennial Cup" game; the OCR clearly identifies it as the "Tier 2 western final" (Abbott Cup stage). No headline is visible in the OCR — the AI-drafted headline appears fabricated. The corrected descriptions reflect the proper tournament context.
